methods.description: Process for analyzing ice core samples using continuous flow analysis to obtain high-resolution records of soluble and insoluble impurities.

steps.description: CFA data were obtained during three measurement campaigns (2018-2020) at the University of Bern using the CFA setup described in Kaufmann et al. (2008) with minor improvements.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain CFA data from measurement campaigns

steps.description: A longitudinal 35 x 35 mm section of the ice core was cut and melted down-core and analysed using continuous measurement methods.
steps.searchable_summary: cut and melt ice core section for analysis

steps.description: Micro-particle concentrations were determined using an Abakus Laser Particle Sizer operating in the size range between 1-15 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: determine micro-particle concentrations using laser particle sizer

steps.description: The depth resolution of the data is limited by signal dispersion in the analytical system to approximately 0.5-1 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: process data with depth resolution limitations

steps.description: Depth co-registration to the thin sections is limited by the accuracy of the depth assignment in the field and is typically on the order of a few mm.
steps.searchable_summary: co-register depth data to thin sections

steps.description: We calculated the arithmetic mean dust particle concentration of each bag for an overview.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate arithmetic mean dust particle concentration
